methods.description: A capture-mark-recapture (CMR) study was conducted to compare the mate-searching behavior of the rare Z. gilesi and the common Z. nigripes. This method allows for the direct comparison of mate searching behavior between the two species, which frequently co-occur and have similar flight phenology.

steps.description: Select study site where Z. gilesi and Z. nigripes co-occur in large numbers.
steps.searchable_summary: select study site based on species co-occurrence

steps.description: Ensure no naturally-occurring Drakaea orchids at the site to avoid interference.
steps.searchable_summary: verify absence of Drakaea orchids at study site

steps.description: Conduct CMR study between 7 October and 3 November 2010.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct CMR study within specified dates

steps.description: Observe male wasps patrolling the study site in search of females.
steps.searchable_summary: observe and record wasp patrolling behavior
